# Product Overview

Explain what the product is, who it serves, and what problem it solves.

Wikiwiki is a local-first documentation companion for code repositories. It is designed for the era where agents are active collaborators inside development environments.

The product stores durable project knowledge as structured records: concepts, decisions, events, notes, symbols, and links. Those records are small enough for agents to maintain and concrete enough for humans to audit. From there, Wikiwiki renders plain Markdown pages and compiles role-oriented human wikis.

The core promise is not "automatic docs." It is more specific: agent-maintained repo memory that can become trustworthy human-readable documentation.

The first user is the coding agent. The second user is the human who needs to understand what happened, why a decision was made, or how to safely continue work.
